# ona_secret (Resource)

Manages a Gitpod secret scoped to a project.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ona_secret" "database_url" {
  name       = "DATABASE_URL"
  value      = "postgres://user:pass@db.example.com/mydb"
  project_id = ona_project.example.id

  environment_variable = true
}

resource "ona_secret" "registry_auth" {
  name       = "REGISTRY_AUTH"
  value      = "dXNlcjpwYXNz"
  project_id = ona_project.example.id

  container_registry_basic_auth_host = "ghcr.io"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable secret name.
- `project_id` (String) Project ID this secret is scoped to.
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Plaintext value of the secret.

### Optional

- `api_only` (Boolean) Whether the secret is only available via API/CLI and not auto-injected.
- `container_registry_basic_auth_host` (String) Registry host for docker config basic auth.
- `environment_variable` (Boolean) Whether the secret is injected as an environment variable with the same name.
- `file_path` (String) Absolute path where the secret is mounted as a file.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the secret was created (RFC3339).
- `creator_id` (String) ID of the subject that created the secret.
- `creator_principal` (String) Principal type of the creator.
- `id` (String) Secret ID.
- `updated_at` (String) Timestamp when the secret was last updated (RFC3339).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import ona_secret.database_url <project-id>/<secret-id>
```
